traceDecision: Required because repository-affecting work on branches starting with fix/ must have an auditable trace.
task: Fix /pages/start/ so the page is owned by the repository GOV.UK Nunjucks renderer and generated Sass pipeline.

selectedBundles.selectionBasis: conditional: GOV.UK page template, generated HTML, form markup and Sass
conditional: Cloudflare Pages publishes committed public/ generated output

skippedBundles.reason: No OpenAI API, model or eval implementation was in scope.
No MCP server, tool, resource, prompt or consent work was in scope.
No Airtable API or data integration work was in scope.
No Mural API or collaboration integration work was in scope.

precedenceDecisions: GitHub Diamond governed branch naming, trace coverage, surgical mutation, validation evidence and PR readiness.
ResearchOps Developer Control governed repository conventions, generated page ownership and route-state test expectations.
Multi-Functional Team governed public-sector service assurance and residual-risk framing.
GOV.UK Design System governed shared frontend layout, form component markup, input width decisions and page affordance checks.
Cloudflare context governed the decision to keep regenerated public/ output in the change set while the deployment contract publishes committed files.

selectedSubAgents.reason: Nunjucks source ownership and renderer registration
Sass source ownership and generated CSS target coverage
Focused validation lane selection and blockers
Changed-file plausibility, trace and PR readiness

generatedOutput.reasonCommitted: Cloudflare Pages currently publishes committed public/ files.
Route stylesheet is committed generated output and guarded by generated-css checks.

validation.summary: Generated all registered CSS targets, including the new start route target.
Rendered GOV.UK pages and regenerated public/pages/start/index.html from src/govuk/templates/pages/start.njk.
Focused start, GOV.UK renderer and Sass migration route-state tests passed.
Trace coverage confirmed for the fix/ branch.
Repository formatting and generated CSS checks passed after ignoring local Sublime workspace files.
Lint passed with existing repository warnings. The generated CSS step produced an out-of-scope Home Office stylesheet diff that was removed from the worktree.
Full local Node test suite passed: 222 tests.
Built GOV.UK assets, generated CSS and GOV.UK pages.
Direct route-state tests passed against committed generated output.
Repository validation completed successfully after route-state assertions were made robust to generated attribute wrapping.
Confirmed page title, H1, project form, four steps, generated GOV.UK CSS, start route CSS and no legacy forms fragment stylesheet.
Focused start page checks passed after addressing the Codex review comment about the hidden utility.

issuesAndPivots.issue: Initial Sass source imported GOV.UK Frontend and generated a full GOV.UK stylesheet into public/css/start.css.
issuesAndPivots.pivot: Kept start.scss as a narrow route stylesheet because the shared layout already loads /assets/govuk/govuk-frontend.css.

issuesAndPivots.issue: Focused tests initially expected legacy GOV.UK fragment styles on /pages/start/.
issuesAndPivots.pivot: Updated tests to assert the new shared layout and full GOV.UK Frontend CSS contract.

issuesAndPivots.issue: Untracked ResearchOps.sublime-workspace was a known formatting and commit hygiene blocker.
issuesAndPivots.pivot: Added *.sublime-workspace to .gitignore and .prettierignore so the editor workspace file remains local.

issuesAndPivots.issue: npm run lint runs the full generated CSS build and temporarily reintroduced an unrelated public/css/brands/home-office.css diff.
issuesAndPivots.pivot: Removed that out-of-scope generated side-effect from the worktree before staging.

issuesAndPivots.issue: Direct validate checks failed on brittle exact-string assertions after generated HTML wrapped attributes.
issuesAndPivots.pivot: Changed the assertions to check ID and class contracts independently.

issuesAndPivots.issue: Codex review noted that the AI assist panels still used the hidden utility after /css/screen.css was removed.
issuesAndPivots.pivot: Added a route-local .hidden display rule to src/styles/start.scss, regenerated public/css/start.css and covered it in tests/start-page-route-state.test.js.

residualRisks: public/pages/start/index.html has a larger generated diff because the page moved from static HTML to the shared Nunjucks renderer.
The worktree contains unrelated local edits to infra/cloudflare/src/core/auth/passwordless.js that must not be staged for this PR.
